message:
refactor(rchd): propagate cancellation field to test fixtures and apply rustfmt (bd-1yt6)

Mechanical update across all rchd test modules that construct
BuildRecord literals: add the new `cancellation: None` field so
the code compiles after the BuildRecord struct gained the optional
cancellation metadata field.

Affected test fixture helpers in:
- admission.rs: make_build_record()
- disk_full_prevention_tests.rs: make_build_record()
- headroom.rs: make_remote_build(), make_local_build()
- main.rs: three inline BuildRecord constructions in daemon
  integration tests
- selection.rs: no structural change, only rustfmt

Additionally, process_triage.rs and reliability.rs receive
extensive rustfmt-only reformatting:
- process_triage.rs: Multi-line method calls, closure formatting,
  argument list alignment, match arm formatting, and chained
  iterator expressions reformatted to comply with default rustfmt
  rules. No logic changes. Applies to both the RemediationPipeline
  implementation and the full test suite (~300 lines reformatted).
- reliability.rs: SignalWeights::normalized() sum expression, method
  signatures, HashMap entry API calls, and test helper invocations
  reformatted. No logic changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
